description: Unit: due-work row builder returns stable execution identity for a client-cadence billing-cycle-backed recurring row.
Unit: due-work row builder returns stable execution identity for a contract-cadence recurring service-period row with no billing-cycle bridge.
Unit: due-work row builder exposes cadence-source metadata, service-period labels, and invoice-window labels for client-cadence rows.
Unit: due-work row builder exposes cadence-source metadata, service-period labels, and invoice-window labels for contract-cadence rows.
Unit: due-work row builder includes contract and contract-line context for contract-cadence recurring windows.
Unit: due-work selection excludes billed recurring service periods from the ready-to-invoice reader.
Unit: due-work selection excludes archived and superseded recurring service periods from the ready-to-invoice reader.
Unit: due-work selection includes a compatibility client-cadence row when no persisted recurring service-period row exists yet.
Integration: due-work reader returns mixed client-cadence and contract-cadence recurring rows for one tenant in a deterministic sort order.
Integration: due-work reader pagination remains stable when rows include a mix of bridged and unbridged recurring windows.
Integration: due-work reader search by client name works for contract-cadence rows with no client billing-cycle record.
Integration: due-work reader date filter operates on invoice-window dates rather than `client_billing_cycles.period_end_date` for contract-cadence rows.
Unit: recurring service-period backfill creates future active records for active recurring obligations with no existing rows.
Unit: recurring service-period replenishment reports missing-horizon state when future rows do not reach the configured target horizon.
Unit: recurring service-period regeneration preserves edited and billed override records while regenerating untouched future records.
Integration: recurring service-period regeneration updates active future rows after a cadence-owner or timing change and preserves billed history.
Migration: widening the recurring service-period participation model allows hourly recurring obligations to be represented in due-work selection.
Migration: widening the recurring service-period participation model allows usage recurring obligations to be represented in due-work selection.
Integration: billed recurring service periods link back to invoice charge detail rows after invoice creation for client-cadence recurring invoices.
Integration: billed recurring service periods link back to invoice charge detail rows after invoice creation for contract-cadence recurring invoices.
Integration: deleting a recurring invoice clears service-period invoice linkage and restores invoiceable lifecycle state for unbridged contract-cadence rows.
Integration: reversing a recurring invoice restores service-period state correctly for a bridged client-cadence row.
Unit: duplicate-prevention identity remains deterministic for a contract-cadence execution window with no billing-cycle bridge.
Integration: duplicate prevention prevents re-invoicing the same contract-cadence execution window even when there is no `billing_cycle_id`.
UI: AutomaticInvoices loads ready rows from the due-work reader instead of `getAvailableBillingPeriods(...)`.
UI: AutomaticInvoices renders a contract-cadence row that has no `billing_cycle_id`.
UI: AutomaticInvoices selection works for a mixed set of client-cadence and contract-cadence rows using execution identity keys.
UI: AutomaticInvoices pagination does not lose selection correctness when row identities are execution-window-based.
UI: AutomaticInvoices shows cadence-source, service-period, and invoice-window columns for contract-cadence rows.
UI: AutomaticInvoices shows contract-name context on contract-cadence rows and does not require a billing-cycle label fallback.
UI: AutomaticInvoices maps generation failures back to a contract-cadence row using execution identity when `billing_cycle_id` is null.
UI: AutomaticInvoices preview opens correctly for a client-cadence row through the selector-input preview path.
UI: AutomaticInvoices preview opens correctly for a contract-cadence row through the selector-input preview path.
UI: AutomaticInvoices batch generate submits selector-input execution windows for unbridged contract-cadence rows.
UI: AutomaticInvoices mixed batch generate submits billing-cycle-backed and unbridged selector-input rows together without losing identity.
UI: AutomaticInvoices batch PO-overage analysis resolves a contract-cadence row with no `billing_cycle_id`.
UI: AutomaticInvoices single-row preview generate path uses selector-input overage and generation for an unbridged contract-cadence row.
UI: preview state survives close/reopen for a selector-input recurring row without relying on `billing_cycle_id`.
UI: contract-cadence due rows show a clear badge or helper text indicating there is no client billing-cycle bridge.
UI: ready-to-invoice view still renders compatibility client-cadence rows during the cutover.
Unit: selector-input preview action validates invoice permissions the same way as the billing-cycle wrapper.
Integration: selector-input preview action returns the same recurring service-period details as billing-cycle preview for an equivalent client-cadence row.
Integration: selector-input preview action returns correct recurring service-period details for a contract-cadence row with no billing-cycle bridge.
Unit: selector-input PO-overage action returns null when the preview spans no PO-governed client contract.
Integration: selector-input PO-overage action computes overage correctly for a contract-cadence execution window.
Integration: recurring generation API accepts a selector-input execution window and creates an invoice with no `billing_cycle_id` bridge.
Integration: recurring generation API accepts a compatibility billing-cycle wrapper and routes internally through the selector-input pipeline.
Unit: preview and generate error payloads include execution identity for unbridged recurring windows.
Integration: preview and generate still enforce billing email validation for selector-input execution windows.
Integration: preview and generate still enforce PO-required contract validation for selector-input execution windows.
Integration: invoiced-history reader returns client-cadence recurring invoices with service-period metadata.
Integration: invoiced-history reader returns contract-cadence recurring invoices with service-period metadata and no billing-cycle bridge.
UI: invoiced recurring history view renders a contract-cadence invoice row and does not depend on `billing_cycle_id` for row actions.
Integration: reversing a contract-cadence recurring invoice updates invoiced history and restores service-period state.
Integration: hard-deleting a contract-cadence recurring invoice updates invoiced history and restores service-period state.
Integration: reversing a client-cadence compatibility invoice preserves the existing billing-cycle-backed behavior while updating service-period linkage.
Integration: hard-deleting a client-cadence compatibility invoice preserves the existing billing-cycle-backed behavior while updating service-period linkage.
UI: recurring invoice reverse/delete actions show correct copy for service-period-backed contract-cadence invoices.
Schema: invoice preview request schema accepts selector-input execution windows.
Schema: recurring invoice generation request schema accepts selector-input execution windows.
API: preview endpoint accepts a selector-input request and returns preview output successfully.
API: generate endpoint accepts a selector-input request and creates a recurring invoice successfully.
API: preview endpoint continues to accept `billing_cycle_id` compatibility requests during the cutover.
API: generate endpoint continues to accept `billing_cycle_id` compatibility requests during the cutover.
API: InvoiceService preview path no longer queries `client_billing_cycles` directly for unbridged contract-cadence requests.
API: invoice list/filter contract can return recurring invoices with execution-window metadata and nullable `billing_cycle_id`.
API: invoice list filter can query recurring invoices by execution-window kind or cadence source when provided.
UI/API compatibility: existing invoice consumers tolerate recurring invoices whose `billing_cycle_id` is null but whose service-period details are present.
Integration: hourly recurring charges bill approved time entries that fall inside a contract-cadence service period, not the nearest client billing cycle.
Integration: hourly recurring charges with no billable time inside the service period produce no recurring invoice line while preserving due-window identity.
Integration: usage recurring charges bill usage records that fall inside a contract-cadence service period, not the nearest client billing cycle.
Integration: usage recurring charges with no usage inside the service period produce no recurring invoice line while preserving due-window identity.
Integration: mixed recurring invoice generation can combine fixed, hourly, and usage content under one service-driven execution window when the commercial model requires it.
Unit: service-driven time and usage billing preserves canonical `servicePeriodStart`, `servicePeriodEnd`, and `billingTiming` metadata on generated charges.
Integration: due-work reader reports a missing-materialization state when a recurring obligation should exist but no future periods have been generated.
Permission: service-period view actions require `billing.recurring_service_periods.view`.
Permission: service-period regenerate actions require `billing.recurring_service_periods.regenerate`.
Permission: service-period repair/history correction actions require `billing.recurring_service_periods.correct_history`.
Integration: service-period regeneration action surfaces conflict payloads when a preserved edited/billed record no longer matches regenerated candidates.
UI: service-period management surface can show future generated rows, edited rows, and billed rows for one recurring obligation.
Workflow: recurring billing run started/completed/failed payloads record `contract_cadence_window` when a run contains only contract-cadence execution windows.
Workflow: recurring billing run payloads retain deterministic selection and retry keys for mixed client-cadence and contract-cadence due-work rows.
UI: recurring generation errors for unbridged rows display execution identity instead of blank client-name keys.
Runbook: backfill and replenishment commands are documented with enough detail to initialize service-driven invoicing in a local or staging tenant.
Runbook: reverse/delete repair workflow is documented for service-driven recurring invoices.
DB-backed integration: full happy path from due-work selection to preview to generate to invoiced-history works for a contract-cadence recurring invoice with no billing-cycle bridge.
DB-backed integration: full happy path from due-work selection to preview to generate to invoiced-history works for a client-cadence compatibility recurring invoice.
DB-backed integration: mixed batch generation from AutomaticInvoices creates both a bridged client-cadence recurring invoice and an unbridged contract-cadence recurring invoice.
DB-backed integration: deleting a contract-cadence recurring invoice makes the same execution window reappear in due-work selection.
DB-backed integration: reversing a bridged client-cadence recurring invoice preserves compatibility behavior and restores due selection appropriately.
Regression: legacy billing-cycle-only AutomaticInvoices flows still work when the selected row is a compatibility client-cadence row.
Regression: invoice list and invoice detail readers still populate service-period summaries for recurring invoices after the service-driven cutover.
Regression: purchase-order overage checks still behave correctly for legacy client-cadence recurring invoices after selector-input overage support is added.
Regression: recurring preview/generate wrappers still return the existing error shape for invalid or unauthorized compatibility billing-cycle requests.
Regression: materialized recurring service-period replenishment does not duplicate active future records when rerun idempotently.
Regression: service-driven invoicing cutover leaves non-recurring manual invoice flows unchanged.
